rulings.Case Title: BEVERLEE EDWARDS et al VS. AC AND S INC et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1. Defendant Union Carbide Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is off calendar. Stephen Hart¿s deposition testimony and declaration, Phillip Hart¿s deposition testimony and declaration, and Mr. Hamilton¿s 2008 trial testimony create a triable issue whether Mrs. Nine was exposed to Hamilton joint compound that contained asbestos attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to Stephen Hart¿s declaration, Phillip Hart¿s declaration and Mr. Hamilton¿s trial testimony are overruled. Plaintiffs dismissed their enterprise liability and false representation causes of action, which were the only issues for which summary adjudication was sought. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LLOYD KENDRICK et al VS. BORGWARNER, INC. INDIVIDUALLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Maremont Corp.¿s motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issue 1 (punitive damages) and off calendar as to issues 2, 3, 4 (enterprise/market share liability, breach of warranty and false representation). As to punitive damages, defendant sustained its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present admissible clear and convincing evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant punitive damages. As to issues 2-4, plaintiffs represent that those claims are being dismissed or were never asserted against defendant. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LLOYD KENDRICK et al VS. BORGWARNER, INC. INDIVIDUALLY AND AS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 3. Defendant Motor Parts Distributors, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment and alternative motion for summary adjudication are continued on the court¿s own motion to July 28, 2011 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 220 to allow defendant to depose Mr. Neidigh. Plaintiffs¿ counsel should attempt to secure Mr. Neidigh voluntarily attendance at a deposition, but must also provide defendant with Mr. Neidigh¿s contact information sufficient to subpoena him. Supplemental reply papers may be filed by defendant but are limited to the deposition of Mr. Neidigh and must be filed and served no later than July 25. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SAM HASTY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Complaint
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s motion to strike is denied in part and off calendar in part. Defendant¿s motion is denied as to agency. The agency allegations are sufficiently pled. Defendant¿s motion is off calendar as to punitive damages, as plaintiffs have dismissed their request for punitive damages. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SAM HASTY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, JUNE 30, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 5. Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc.¿s demurrer to first amended complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath. = (220/HEK)
